text.title: devops-platform-backstage Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for devops-platform-backstage as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Deploying Backstage without dedicated engineering capacity to maintain it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The catalog goes stale within weeks without automated entity discovery and active catalog maintenance. Stale catalog data (wrong owners, missing services) destroys developer trust in 2-3 months, making adoption near-impossible to recover.

failure-mode.description: Building custom Backstage plugins for integrations that already have community plugins.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 300+ community plugins exist in 2026. Custom plugin development takes 4-8 weeks; adopting a community plugin takes 1-2 days. Always check the Backstage plugin marketplace before building.

failure-mode.description: Using Backstage as the infrastructure orchestration layer instead of the portal/catalog layer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Backstage is a portal, not an orchestrator. Complex infrastructure provisioning (multi-resource, multi-cloud) belongs in Crossplane or Terraform pipelines. Backstage Software Templates should invoke those backends, not implement the provisioning logic themselves.
